result:
Done — the spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly with zero warnings, using 1 of the 10 build cycles.

The project turned out to be written in Calor (a contract-first DSL that compiles to C# via a custom MSBuild SDK), so I added the three functions to `WindowStats.calr` following the conventions of the existing `WindowSum`:

- **`WindowMin` (f002)** — same three preconditions as `WindowSum` (`start >= 0`, `count > 0`, `start + count <= len`), seeds `best` with the first window element and scans the rest, so negative elements are handled naturally.
- **`MaxWindowSum` (f003)** — preconditions `count > 0` and `count <= len`; seeds `best` with the window at index 0 (so an all-negative array yields the correct negative maximum) and reuses `WindowSum` for each subsequent window.
- **`CountAbove` (f004)** — precondition `count > 0` plus a postcondition `§S (>= result 0)` for the never-negative guarantee; returns 0 early when `count` exceeds the array length, otherwise counts windows whose sum is strictly greater than the threshold.

I verified the generated C# in `obj/calor/` — the emitted code matches the spec's semantics exactly (strict `>` comparison in `CountAbove`, all-windows scan bounded by `s <= values.Length - count`, contracts emitted as runtime checks), and `WindowSum` is untouched.
